training_tokens.as_published: 299,892,736,000 tokens = 143,000 steps x 2,097,152-token batch; 'just under 1 epoch' on the (non-deduplicated) Pile

ac_config_adaptations: GELU MLP -> SwiGLU encoding: AC's dense FFN schema/loader accepts only swiglu (a 3-matrix FFN, 3*d*ffn params). Pythia's true FFN is a 2-matrix GELU MLP (2*d*8192 params). Encoding it verbatim as swiglu ffn_dim=8192 would inflate AC's parameter ledger ~28% (1.82B vs the published 1.415B) and bias the Chinchilla spine N. We therefore encode the param-equivalent width ffn_dim = round(2/3 * 8192) = 5461, so 3*2048*5461 = 33,552,384 ~ 2*2048*8192 = 33,554,432. AC ledger total: 1,414,053,888 vs published 1,414,647,808 (-0.04%). The architecture_residual MLP/attn-ratio term has weight 0.0 in stock constants, so the narrower ffn_dim does not move shape penalties.
rotary_pct 0.25 (Pythia rotates only 25% of head dims) is not representable in AC's schema; config records rope with base 10000. No AC quality term reads rotary_pct.

parallelism.tensor_parallel = 1: the param-equivalent ffn_dim 5461 = 43x127 has no power-of-2 divisor, and AC requires TP | ffn_dim. TP affects only throughput metrics; the quality prediction (this study's target) is TP-invariant.
metadata.params declares AC-ledger-consistent values (1.41B); published counts recorded above.

prediction_inputs.note: 'Just under 1 epoch' -> repetition ~0, unique tokens left null (D_eff = D). Evaluation context == pretraining context so context_utility is identically zero.
